how: These activity cards are automatically generated based on activity in my memory system. I review them every few days, and they automatically publish here.

items.title: Packaged an internal skill as a marketplace plugin
items.body: Packaged a canonical agent skill as a marketplace plugin so it installs once and version-updates cleanly for a whole team, instead of everyone hand-copying stale files. Set it up as a one-way sync from the source of truth, byte-identical, with a version bump that triggers the update on install.

items.title: Rebuilt a static calculator as a design-system route
items.body: Converted a standalone HTML deal calculator into a native React route that consumes a design system at the component level, so it tracks the system's tokens, shapes and type instead of carrying its own hand-picked CSS. Ported the calculation logic across untouched, swapped every surface to real Card, Slider and Button components with a class-based dark and light toggle, and verified it rendered clean before publishing.

items.title: Built a synthetic-user research skill that fights AI flattery
items.body: Built a reusable research skill that runs synthetic-user focus groups without the usual AI sycophancy, forcing the model to lead with objections and to tag every claim as grounded, inferred or assumed rather than dressing hypotheses up as insight.

items.title: Wired a primitive guard into a design system
items.body: Added a commit-time guard hook to a design system that blocks hand-rolled dialogs, tooltips and tabs and forces the shared primitive instead, so drift cannot creep in component by component. Tested it both ways, a hand-rolled component trips it and the proper import clears it, then wrote the rule into the contributor guide.

items.title: Built a drop-in scroll-animation tool for static pages
items.body: Built a tool that takes any static HTML page and writes an animated copy, injecting GSAP scroll-reveal on the content blocks plus hover polish on cards and buttons, without touching the original file. Made it safe by default, so no-JS, load failures and reduced-motion preferences all fall back to fully visible content rather than hiding anything behind an animation that never runs.

items.title: Rebalanced the type scale in a design system
items.body: Reworked the UI type scale in a design system, bringing functional controls down a notch to a macOS-style 13px base for density while leaving editorial reading text at its larger size. Unified the mono onto one foundry, pulled secondary actions back to a single action colour, and added a hair of positive tracking at small sizes so the interface holds up as it shrinks.

items.title: Stood up the design foundation for a rate-comparison product
items.body: Built and deployed a token kit for a rate-comparison product, a componentised shadcn front end ready to hand to a dev team to wire up to live data. Anchored colour, type and spacing as reusable tokens and pulled the base toward a more authoritative tone, so the pages read sharp and trustworthy rather than poppy.

items.title: Built an internal hub wired to a design system
items.body: Stood up a private internal site that consumes a design system at the component level, so a change to the system flows straight through to the live pages instead of being copied across by hand. Got the propagation test passing, then set up the repo ready to deploy.
